symbols.name: detectScatters
symbols.detail: [USED] Runtime-imported by 1 file: src/engine.ts | [UNIQUE] No similar functions found in codebase | [OK] Correctly counts all SCATTER symbols across every reel cell, consistent with documented 5×3 full-grid scatter counting. | [LEAN] Simple nested loop counting SCATTER symbols across the grid. Exactly what the docs specify, no unnecessary abstraction. | [NONE] No test file exists. Function is used in engine.ts for core game logic but has zero test coverage across all paths: empty reels, no scatters, multiple scatters, scatter in multiple columns. | [UNDOCUMENTED] No JSDoc comment. Missing description of purpose, parameter shape (2D readonly array), and return semantics (total SCATTER count across all reels).

symbols.name: handleFreeSpins
symbols.detail: [USED] Runtime-imported by 1 file: src/engine.ts | [UNIQUE] No similar functions found in codebase | [OK] All three branches (initial trigger, retrigger, decrement/deactivate) match documented invariants: first trigger sets remaining=10, retrigger adds 10, normal spin decrements; deactivation fires correctly at remaining≤0. | [LEAN] Flat if/else chain implementing the three documented state transitions (trigger, retrigger, decrement). No abstraction beyond what the logic requires. | [NONE] No test file exists. Six distinct branches (initial activation, re-trigger, decrement, deactivation, inactive+<3 scatters, active+<3 scatters approaching zero) are all untested despite being critical game state mutations. | [UNDOCUMENTED] No JSDoc comment. Non-trivial state-mutation logic with three distinct branches (trigger, retrigger, decrement/deactivate) warrants documentation of parameters, side effects on FreeSpinState, and the threshold/award constants.

actions.description: Add JSDoc documentation for exported symbol: `detectScatters`
Add JSDoc documentation for exported symbol: `handleFreeSpins`

file_level.general_notes: Best practices score: 8.75/10 (1 FAIL, 2 WARN, 14 PASS)

rules.detail: No implicit-any, no implicit-return, no loose assignments visible. Cannot verify tsconfig from this file, but the code itself is consistent with strict mode.
No explicit or implicit `any`. All parameters and return types are fully annotated.
No type assertions. Boolean guards on `state.active` are appropriate for this two-state machine.
`ReadonlyArray<ReadonlyArray<Symbol>>` used correctly in `detectScatters`. No obvious missed opportunities for Pick/Omit/Partial.

`detectScatters` input is `ReadonlyArray<ReadonlyArray<Symbol>>`. `handleFreeSpins` intentionally mutates its `FreeSpinState` argument — this is the documented design per `.anatoly/docs/02-Architecture/02-Core-Concepts.md`.
Imports use `import type` consistently. `FreeSpinState` is an `interface` per internal docs, aligning with project convention.

Magic number literals `3` and `10` appear inline in `handleFreeSpins` (L14, L16, L17). ESLint `no-magic-numbers` would flag these. The values are documented in `.anatoly/docs/03-Guides/02-Advanced-Configuration.md` but named constants would be safer against accidental drift.
Both exported functions `detectScatters` and `handleFreeSpins` lack JSDoc. Both are public API surface (referenced in internal docs). Param types and return semantics should be documented.
No deprecated APIs. `for...of` over index loops, `import type` used. No issues.
Single grouped `import type` from a local module. No side-effect or circular imports.
Both functions are fully synchronous. No async paths, no unhandled rejections.

Slot-machine domain confirmed by SCATTER/free-spin vocabulary and rng.ts/jackpot.ts in project structure. No `Math.random()` in this file (isolated to `rng.ts`). No hardcoded secrets, no eval, no command injection.
`detectScatters` is O(reels × rows) — appropriate for a fixed 5×3 grid. No unnecessary allocations.

`detectScatters` is a pure function. `handleFreeSpins` mutates its argument and returns `void`, requiring callers to set up mutable state objects and assert side effects. A variant returning a new `FreeSpinState` would allow pure unit tests.
File is simple enough that `satisfies`, const type params, or `using` add no value here. No missed opportunities.

Slot-engine utility file. No React, no HTTP, no streaming. Free-spin logic matches documented invariants in `.anatoly/docs/04-API-Reference/02-Configuration-Schema.md` and `.anatoly/docs/02-Architecture/02-Core-Concepts.md`.

suggestions.description: Extract magic numbers into named constants to prevent drift and satisfy no-magic-numbers lint rule
Add JSDoc to both public exports
Consider a pure variant of handleFreeSpins that returns a new state for easier unit testing
